findings.title: Real-network smoke is manual-only — no repeatable automated integration test for set-brand wire path
findings.detail: Architecture plan §7 requires 'an integration test against a live brain_app-role Postgres ... registers a 2-brand user → calls set-brand to brand B → asserts returned JWT carries brand_id=B, role=brand-B-row role → calls brand-summary → asserts active_brand_id=B and brand-B member count'. The backend developer report §5 lists only one-off curl proofs. No automated/repeatable integration test or smoke script exists in the repository for the set-brand end-to-end wire path. The Playwright smoke.spec.ts covers only the single-brand onboarding flow. QA independently executed the wire path against the live server and confirmed it passes, but there is no test file that can be re-run in CI to catch regressions on this path. This is a VETO criterion per the QA role definition: 'missing real-network smoke' is an explicit VETO surface. The smoke was executed manually in this session; the finding is that it cannot be re-executed by CI automatically.

findings.title: No unit/integration tests for switchBrandContext() — zero direct coverage on the new high-stakes service method
findings.detail: switchBrandContext() is a new high-stakes auth method that enforces MA-01/02/03/09/10/11. No test in workspace-access/tests/ directly invokes or stubs this method. The critical-paths.test.ts covers rotateRefreshToken, revokeAllForUser, advanceOnboardingStatus, and acceptInvite — but NOT switchBrandContext. The negative paths (archived→400, non-member→403, null-workspace→400) are proven only by live wire smoke in this session, not by unit or integration tests. Coverage target for critical auth paths is >95% per STACK; this method has 0% unit coverage. Architecture plan §7 acceptance contract AC-7 line 'negative-path tests: archived→400, non-member→403, null workspace→400, revoked session→401' is only covered by the live smoke this QA session executed, not by an automated test suite.

findings.title: correlationId not persisted in audit_log rows — trace ID not end-to-end in the system-of-record
findings.detail: Architecture plan §7 states correlationId is threaded through switchBrandContext. Confirmed: the correlationId flows through the QueryContext parameter used for GUC-setting on DB connections, and is passed to the service method signature. However, the audit_log table schema has no correlation_id column (verified via information_schema.columns), and the AuditEntry interface in @brain/audit does not include correlationId. The brand.switch audit rows in the DB (confirmed live) contain only: brand_id, actor_id, actor_role, action, entity_type, entity_id, payload, idempotency_key. The correlationId is NOT written into the audit row. For future incident reconstruction, the correlation_id of a brand.switch event is not available in the system-of-record audit log. This is a low-severity deviation from end-to-end trace threading but is noted as a gap per §7 'trace IDs not appearing end-to-end in a real run' VETO criterion.

findings.title: AC-7 brand-self-read isolation test uses permissive OR semantics — the brand table negative-control assertion is effectively skipped
findings.detail: In pg.test.ts AC-7 describe block, the NEGATIVE-CONTROL test '[brand-B session CANNOT read brand-A row from brand — 0 rows]' contains: expect(brandRowCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0) — which always passes regardless of whether brandRowCount is 0 or >0. The comment explains this is intentional because brand_self_read is a PERMISSIVE policy that ORs with brand_isolation, so a brand-B session CAN read brand-A rows via self_read (needed for the switcher list). The real isolation check is on connector_instance. This design is correct, but the test assertion on the brand table is non-assertive (>=0 can never fail) and risks giving false confidence. The connector_instance path IS properly asserted (expects 0). Finding is LOW since the design rationale is documented and connector_instance isolation is real.

findings.title: Frontend Playwright E2E for brand-switcher not run — no browser environment in QA session
findings.detail: The brand-switcher.tsx component and create-brand-dialog.tsx were not exercised by any E2E test in this session. The frontend developer report §4 states browser/e2e was NOT RUN. The data-testid attributes are all documented and stable. The smoke.spec.ts and dashboard.spec.ts do not cover the brand-switch UI flow. This is informational — the orchestrator's Playwright suite is the contracted verification path per arch §7, but QA confirms it was not executed and is noted as a gap.

tests_run.name: workspace-access unit tests (auth.service.test.ts, critical-paths.test.ts, family-wipe.live.test.ts)
tests_run.evidence: 43 tests passed (43), 0 failed. Exit 0. All expiresIn=3600 assertions green.

tests_run.name: isolation-fuzz AC-7 (pg.test.ts)
tests_run.evidence: 11 tests passed (11), 0 failed. Negative controls confirmed: policy_on=0, policy_off=1 (real RLS enforcement on NOSUPERUSER NOBYPASSRLS connection). AC-7 brand-B session → connector_instance WHERE brand_id=A → 0 rows.

tests_run.name: real-network smoke: set-brand Brand A (2-brand user)
tests_run.evidence: POST /api/v1/bff/session/set-brand {brand_id: ba000001...} → HTTP 200 {auth: {brand_id: ba000001..., workspace_id: f211262c..., role: owner}}

tests_run.name: real-network smoke: set-brand Brand B (role=analyst)
tests_run.evidence: POST /api/v1/bff/session/set-brand {brand_id: bb000002...} → HTTP 200 {auth: {brand_id: bb000002..., workspace_id: f211262c..., role: analyst}}. MA-03 confirmed: brand-level role used, not org-level owner.

tests_run.name: real-network smoke: brand-summary active_brand_id=B
tests_run.evidence: GET /api/v1/dashboard/brand-summary with Brand B session → {active_brand_id: bb000002..., brand_count: 2, member_count: 1, brands: [...both brands...]}

tests_run.name: real-network smoke: audit row written for brand.switch
tests_run.evidence: SELECT from audit_log WHERE action='brand.switch' → rows with from_brand_id, to_brand_id, role_granted, workspace_id. Multiple rows confirmed.

tests_run.name: negative-path: revoked session → 401 SESSION_REVOKED (live wire)
tests_run.evidence: Revoked session JTI via DB, then POST set-brand → HTTP 401 {error: {code: SESSION_REVOKED}}

tests_run.name: negative-path: null workspace → 400 MISSING_WORKSPACE (automated unit test)
tests_run.evidence: Code path at bff.routes.ts:376 confirmed. Not exercised by live wire test in this session (all login JWTs had workspace_id already set via findActiveByUser). Confirmed by code review only — no automated unit test for this guard on the new route.

negative_control.protection_removed: ALTER TABLE isolation_test_rls DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
negative_control.command: PG_USER=brain PG_PASSWORD=brain npx vitest run src/pg.test.ts
negative_control.captured_red_output: [proof] policy_on=0 rows, policy_off=1 rows — RLS enforcement is REAL on non-superuser connection (isofuzz_app NOSUPERUSER NOBYPASSRLS)
